source.catalog_ref: HV24 (user-assigned national aerospace student benchmark Hyperloop Development + BNL MagLev; catalog HV23 national aerospace student benchmark + HV24 BNL MagLev)

full_prompt: Design a monolithic 6061-T6 aluminum passive-magnet levitation vehicle chassis satisfying both the Brookhaven Lab MagLev Competition 2026 high-school rules (16 ft / 4.88 m track with 2 ft / 0.61 m total drop, 2.5 in / 63.5 mm magnet channel, 1 in x 0.75 in ceramic magnets spaced 1 in center-to-center, vehicle <=16 in / 406 mm long, gravity-powered descent) and satisfying the national aerospace student benchmark 2026 Hyperloop Development Competition Tech-Demo track requirement for a reduced-scale maglev demonstration vehicle. The chassis is a single billet-machined 6061-T6 part (no welds, no bolted-on tabs; integral central spine + integral magnet-carrier rails + integral payload cradle + integral front/rear bumper-stops). Geometry: 400 mm long x 60 mm wide x 20 mm tall central spine; two integral magnet-carrier rails along the underside of the spine, each 400 mm long x 20 mm wide x 4 mm thick with a 18 mm wide x 5 mm deep channel to hold a row of 20 x (1 in x 0.75 in x 0.5 in) N42 ceramic magnets spaced at 20 mm pitch (repelling the track magnet array); rail gap between the two rails 25 mm (matching the 63.5 mm track channel with some clearance). Integral payload cradle on top of the spine: 100 mm long x 40 mm wide x 10 mm deep trough with 4 integral M4 (4.5 mm clearance) tie-down holes. Integral bumper stops at front and rear: each 30 mm wide x 15 mm tall x 4 mm thick vertical plate. Overall bounding envelope 400 x 60 x 30 mm. Material 6061-T6 (E 68.9 GPa, nu 0.33, density 2700 kg/m^3, yield 276 MPa, UTS 310 MPa, endurance 97 MPa at 5e8 cycles); note: the magnets and the payload are non-structural for this prompt and modeled as lumped masses. Three load cases per combined BNL MagLev + national aerospace student benchmark Tech-Demo practice. Load Case 1 (peak descent dynamics at end of 16 ft track): vehicle total mass (chassis 0.35 kg + magnets 20 x 3.5 g = 70 g + payload 50 g) = 0.47 kg; peak velocity after 0.61 m drop = sqrt(2 * 9.81 * 0.61) = 3.46 m/s; entering the level run-out requires 0.5g lateral correction at the bumper stops + 1.5g longitudinal decel on the rear bumper hitting the track endstop = 3.5 N longitudinal + 2.3 N lateral; applied at the bumpers, magnet-carrier rails constrained by distributed levitation forces. Load Case 2 (end-of-track bumper hit): 25 N longitudinal impulse on the rear bumper over 10 ms (kinetic energy 2.8 J absorbed by elastomer-bumper pad modeled as stiffness BC), chassis fully supported by magnet-carrier levitation. Load Case 3 (drop from 0.3 m on payload install): 0.47 kg x (9.81) x (12/(2*0.005)) dynamic factor = 553 N shock vertical on payload cradle representing a careless install drop onto a hard bench (elastomer pad on bench has 5 mm compression at impact), chassis inverted constrained at bumpers. Pass/fail: (R1) under LC1 peak descent dynamics, peak von Mises must not exceed 138 MPa (yield/2.0); (R2) under LC2 bumper hit, peak stress must not exceed 184 MPa (yield/1.5 transient); (R3) under LC3 install drop shock, peak stress must not exceed 184 MPa; (R4) first six natural frequencies of the chassis (with magnets + payload lumped mass) must exceed 40 Hz to avoid resonance with the 10-30 Hz levitation magnet-track interaction ripple (track magnets spaced at 1 in produce spatial forcing at velocity v/d = 3.46/0.0254 = 136 Hz primary, but include 0.1x subharmonic floor at 13.6 Hz); (R5) chassis mass must not exceed 0.35 kg (keeps total vehicle <= 0.5 kg for BNL small-ceramic-magnet class); (R6) flatness of the magnet-carrier rail channels must not deviate by more than 0.2 mm under LC1 (deviation above 0.2 mm increases levitation gap variability and crashes the vehicle into the track); (R7) overall vehicle length in the chassis direction must remain <= 406 mm (BNL <=16 in limit). Thermal, fluid, buckling, and radiation analyses are not required. Deliver STEP AP242 geometry plus report.
short_prompt:
Design a monolithic 6061-T6 aluminum passive-magnet levitation vehicle chassis for both the Brookhaven Lab MagLev Competition (16 ft / 4.88 m track, 0.61 m drop, 63.5 mm magnet channel, <=16 in vehicle length) and a national aerospace student benchmark Hyperloop Tech-Demo scaled maglev demonstrator. The chassis is a single billet-machined part with a central spine, two integral magnet-carrier rails with channels for 20 N42 ceramic magnets on 20 mm pitch, an integral payload cradle with four tie-down holes, and integral front/rear bumper stops. Apply yield/2.0 continuous and yield/1.5 transient safety factors.

Envelope: 400 x 60 x 30 mm (<=406 mm per BNL rule).

Load cases.
- LC1 peak descent dynamics at run-out: 0.47 kg vehicle at 3.46 m/s, 0.5 g lateral at bumper stops + 1.5 g longitudinal decel = 3.5 N longitudinal + 2.3 N lateral at the bumpers, magnet-carrier rails constrained by distributed levitation forces.
- LC2 end-of-track bumper hit: 25 N longitudinal impulse over 10 ms on the rear bumper (2.8 J via elastomer-bumper stiffness BC), chassis supported by levitation.
- LC3 0.3 m install drop: 553 N vertical shock on the payload cradle (elastomer bench pad 5 mm compression), chassis inverted at the bumpers.
Also require first six modes >40 Hz (decouple 10-30 Hz levitation ripple; 136 Hz primary at 3.46 m/s / 1 in pitch) and magnet-rail channel flatness deviation under LC1 below 0.2 mm.

prompt.title: national aerospace student benchmark + BNL maglev small-vehicle chassis
prompt.brief: 6061-T6 400x60x20 spine chassis w/ dual magnet-carrier rails + payload cradle + bumpers; 16 ft / 2 ft drop BNL track.

exclusions_explained.fluid_analysis: Aero negligible at 3.5 m/s on 0.4 m part.

eval_coverage: BNL HS 16 ft / 2 ft drop envelope
national aerospace student benchmark Tech-Demo mass + rail-flatness
